text.title: elicitation-techniques Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for elicitation-techniques. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Prepare a written interview guide with objectives, question types (open, closed, probing, clarifying), and pre-read materials. Send the guide 24 hours ahead.
rule.rationale: Pre-reading produces 30-50% deeper sessions; unprepared stakeholders give surface-level answers that require follow-up interviews.

rule.statement: Cap interviews at 60 minutes. Past that, signal-to-noise collapses.
rule.rationale: Fatigue produces unreliable notes and stakeholders disengage from the process.

rule.statement: Use anonymous dot-voting or similar mechanisms to prevent the loudest voice from dominating. Record per-vote tallies, not names.
rule.rationale: Workshop dynamics get hijacked without structured participation controls; consensus produced by loudest voice is not consensus.

rule.statement: Cap workshops at 90 minutes with a hard break.
rule.rationale: Past 90 minutes, participant attention degrades and minutes become unreliable.

rule.statement: Pair observation with at least one other technique (interview or document analysis) for every requirement area.
rule.rationale: Stated needs (interviews) differ from actual behavior (observation); skipping observation produces requirements that pass review and fail in production.

rule.statement: Treat every document-analysis claim as "as-documented" and cross-check against at least one observation or interview before promoting to a requirement.
rule.rationale: Documentation typically lags actual implementation by 6-18 months; as-documented state is not as-implemented state.

rule.statement: Surveys with less than 30% response rate are not data — they are anecdotes. Flag response rate in the synthesis report.
rule.rationale: Low-response-rate surveys mislead with statistical framing; the real finding is that the stakeholder group did not engage.

rule.statement: Design with a target N and confidence interval before distributing. Pre-register the analysis plan to prevent post-hoc fishing.
rule.rationale: Open-ended analysis produces artifacts that confirm pre-existing beliefs rather than surface new requirements.

rule.statement: Never use prototyping as the first elicitation technique. Use only after interviews and/or observation have produced candidate requirements.
rule.rationale: Prototyping anchors stakeholders on the prototype's affordances; they stop imagining alternatives and stop surfacing underlying needs.

rule.statement: Every requirement must cite at least two distinct session IDs of different techniques before promotion from candidate to draft.
rule.rationale: Single-source requirements lack triangulation; contradictions between sources are where the real requirements live.

rule.statement: Capture consent in the frontmatter (`consent: true`, `consent_basis: email|signed|verbal+recorded`). No consent → no commit.
rule.rationale: Stakeholder consent is a legal and ethical requirement; missing consent invalidates the entire session artifact.

rule.statement: Redact PII at ingest, not later — Presidio / spaCy NER pass before the transcript hits git. Keep raw recordings outside the repo (S3 with lifecycle policy).
rule.rationale: PII in git is impossible to expunge; redact at the source, not in a post-hoc pass.

rule.statement: Distinguish *stated*, *observed*, and *inferred* needs in the synthesis output. Tag each REQ with its source tier; observed > stated > inferred for credibility.
rule.rationale: The source of a requirement affects its credibility; inferred requirements without observation backing should be flagged for stakeholder validation.

rule.statement: Cross-link every elicitation artifact to a `stakeholder-analysis/` ID and a `ba-planning/` deliverable; otherwise the corpus becomes unsearchable within a sprint.
rule.rationale: Artifacts scattered across the project without cross-references become orphaned; linkage keeps them connected to the broader requirements lifecycle.

rule.statement: Keep an "open questions" log per session that the next session is expected to close. Track close-rate as a process metric.
rule.rationale: Unclosed questions are the leading cause of incomplete requirements; tracking them as a metric highlights discovery thoroughness.

rule.statement: Recording retention policy lives next to the artifact (`retention: 365d`); a cron job purges past-due recordings and logs the deletion. GDPR-friendly by default.
rule.rationale: Raw recordings contain sensitive information; automatic expiry is simpler than manual deletion and proves compliance.

rule.statement: Run a "what did we miss" round at the end of every session — single highest-yield question, almost always uncovers an unspoken constraint.
rule.rationale: This one question consistently surfaces requirements that all other techniques missed; it is the most cost-effective discovery technique per minute spent.
